title: Neural Networks I — Perceptrons
description: The perceptron: a single artificial neuron that computes a weighted sum and applies an activation function. Covers the decision boundary, the perceptron learning rule, and linear separability.
objectives: Describe a perceptron as a weighted sum of inputs plus a bias, passed through an activation function
Compute the weighted sum (pre-activation) and apply the step and sigmoid activations
Interpret a perceptron geometrically as a decision boundary that splits the input space
Apply the perceptron learning rule to update weights from a misclassified example
Explain linear separability and why a single perceptron cannot solve XOR
